mnemonics: Pandas Reads Files Easily, Delivering Organized DataFrames (P.R.F.E.D.O.D.) - a mnemonic for the common file reading functions: pd.read_csv(), pd.read_excel(), pd.read_json(), etc.
keyPoints: Use pandas functions like pd.read_csv() for tabular data, specifying parameters like 'sep' for delimiters and 'header' for row labels.
Always check the data types of imported columns using .dtypes and handle missing values represented by placeholders like NaN or custom symbols.
Set the correct encoding (e.g., encoding='utf-8') when reading text files to avoid character errors, especially with non-ASCII characters.
commonMistakes: Assuming the first row is always the header, leading to mislabeled columns if the file has no headers or a different header row.
Not specifying the correct delimiter (like 'sep' for read_csv) for non-comma separated files (e.g., tabs or spaces), causing parsing errors.
Ignoring the index column during read, resulting in an unwanted Unnamed: 0 column if the file has an index, or not setting a useful index with index_col.
